injuries_home: No reported Argentina injuries. Sports Mole lists Argentina out: none and doubtful: none; Sportsgambler says no Argentina players are currently reported injured or suspended. AS reports Lionel Scaloni said Cristian Romero is basically fine/available if training goes well.
injuries_away: Telmo Arcanjo is listed as doubtful with a muscle issue by Sports Mole. Sportsgambler lists no Cape Verde players currently reported injured or suspended, so other Cape Verde injury information is unknown.
suspensions_home: No reported Argentina suspensions. Sportsgambler states no Argentina players are currently reported injured or suspended.
suspensions_away: No reported Cape Verde suspensions. Sportsgambler states no Cape Verde players are currently reported injured or suspended.
expected_lineup_home: Argentina predicted XI, 4-4-2: Emiliano Martinez; Nahuel Molina, Cristian Romero, Lisandro Martinez, Facundo Medina; Rodrigo De Paul, Alexis Mac Allister, Enzo Fernandez, Thiago Almada; Lionel Messi, Lautaro Martinez.
expected_lineup_away: Cape Verde predicted XI, 4-1-4-1: Vozinha; Steven Moreira, Roberto Lopes/Pico, Diney Borges, Sidny Lopes Cabral; Kevin Lenini; Ryan Mendes, Deroy Duarte, Jamiro Monteiro, Jovane Cabral; Dailon Rocha Livramento. Sources differ between Roberto Lopes and Pico in central defence.
coach_comments_home: Lionel Scaloni warned Argentina not to treat Cape Verde as an easy opponent, saying they are unbeaten, not in the knockout stage by chance, defend well, close inside channels, and counter-attack well. He also said Messi's minutes would depend on match context and how Messi feels.
coach_comments_away: Cape Verde coach Bubista said Cape Verde earned their place on merit, have nothing to fear, see the Argentina match as the match of their lives, and are focused on trying to advance past the round.

injuries_home: Mathew Leckie and Jacob Italiano have left the Australia squad after hamstring and adductor injuries respectively; Socceroos reported no further Australia injury concerns.
injuries_away: Mohamed Salah had hamstring fatigue/strain but was reported available, with Hossam Hassan saying he would not risk him unless fully fit. Ahmed Fatouh has a hamstring tear and Mohamed Abdelmonem has ankle bruising/injury. Other Egypt injury doubts are not treated as confirmed here.
suspensions_home: No Australia suspensions clearly reported in the sources used.
suspensions_away: Mohanad Lasheen is suspended after picking up a milestone yellow card against Iran.
expected_lineup_home: Possible Australia lineup: Patrick Beach; Alessandro Circati, Harry Souttar, Lucas Herrington; Jordan Bos, Aiden O'Neill, Jackson Irvine, Aziz Behich; Cristian Volpato, Nestory Irankunda, Connor Metcalfe.
expected_lineup_away: Possible Egypt lineup: Mostafa Shobeir; Mohamed Hany, Yasser Ibrahim, Rami Rabia, Karim Hafez; Marwan Ateya, Mahmoud Saber; Mostafa Ziko, Mohamed Salah, Emam Ashour; Omar Marmoush.
coach_comments_home: Tony Popovic said Australia had prepared for both scenarios regarding Mohamed Salah playing or not playing, and said Australia have another chance to show they deserve to be at this stage.
coach_comments_away: Hossam Hassan said Salah is available but will not be risked unless fully capable of playing; he also said Egypt have confidence in the squad despite Mohanad Lasheen's suspension and injuries to Ahmed Fatouh and Mohamed Abdelmonem.

injuries_home: Mathew Leckie and Jacob Italiano are reported unavailable/ruled out for Australia; reports cite Leckie hamstring issue and Italiano adductor/muscle injury.
injuries_away: Mohamed Salah had hamstring fatigue/strain against Iran but returned to training and was reported available, with his start uncertain. Ahmed Fatouh is reported injured with a hamstring tear. Mohamed Abdelmonem is reported an injury doubt with an ankle issue/severe bruising. Hossam Abdelmaguid and Hamdy Fathy were also described by Sports Illustrated as doubts, though Football365 said Abdelmaguid should return and Fathy was back in training.
suspensions_away: Mohanad Lasheen is reported suspended for one match after two yellow cards in the group stage.
expected_lineup_home: Australia expected/projected lineup: Patrick Beach; Alessandro Circati, Harry Souttar, Lucas Herrington; Jordan Bos, Jackson Irvine, Aiden O'Neill, Aziz Behich; Cristian Volpato, Connor Metcalfe; Nestory Irankunda.
expected_lineup_away: Egypt expected/projected lineup: Mostafa Ahmed Shobeir; Mohamed Hany, Yasser Ibrahim, Rami Rabia or Hamdi Fathi, Karim Hafez; Marwan Ateya, Mahmoud Saber; Mostafa Ziko, Mohamed Salah, Emam Ashour or Trezeguet; Omar Marmoush. Sources differ on one centre-back/attacking-midfield slot.
coach_comments_home: Tony Popovic said Australia had prepared for both scenarios regarding Salah playing or not playing, and said Australia felt relaxed, confident and prepared. He also said the team must stay in the moment because history is made after the game.
coach_comments_away: Hossam Hassan said Salah is available but would not be risked unless fully capable of playing. He said Egypt have confidence in the squad despite Lasheen's suspension and injuries, and described Australia as well-organized, fast and dangerous on set pieces.

injuries_home: Luca Jaquez was reported out of the Round of 32 match with a muscle injury; earlier pre-match reports also listed Silvan Widmer as a doubt with hip discomfort, but Widmer was later named among Switzerland's substitutes.
injuries_away: Mohamed Amoura was reported still out with a thigh injury; other previews described him as a serious doubt or ruled out.
expected_lineup_home: Confirmed Switzerland XI: Kobel; Zakaria, Elvedi, Akanji; Rodriguez, Xhaka, Freuler, Ndoye; Manzambi, Vargas; Embolo.
expected_lineup_away: Confirmed Algeria XI: Zidane; Belghali, Mandi, Bensebaini, Ait-Nouri; Zerrouki, Bentaleb; Mahrez, Aouar, Chaibi; Maza.
coach_comments_home: Murat Yakin said Switzerland needed to respect every opponent, expected a difficult match, and stressed creating and converting chances with conviction and quality.
coach_comments_away: Vladimir Petkovic said Algeria had used Plans A and B in the group stage, might use Plan C against Switzerland, and that despite being Swiss his country for the match would be Algeria.

injuries_home: Silvan Widmer was a fitness doubt with a hip issue; Sports Mole listed Switzerland 'Out: None' and 'Doubtful: Silvan Widmer (hip)'. Blick also reported Widmer had missed team training with a hip problem, Gregor Kobel had shoulder discomfort but returned to training, and Miro Muheim was recovered.
injuries_away: Mohamed El Amine Amoura was a fitness doubt; Sports Mole listed Algeria 'Out: None' and 'Doubtful: Mohamed El Amine Amoura (unspecified)'. Football.fr reported Amoura had a thigh injury, had not resumed collective training, and had missed Algeria's previous two group matches.
expected_lineup_home: Confirmed XI: Gregor Kobel; Denis Zakaria, Nico Elvedi, Manuel Akanji, Ricardo Rodriguez; Dan Ndoye, Granit Xhaka, Remo Freuler; Ruben Vargas, Breel Embolo, Johan Manzambi.
expected_lineup_away: Confirmed XI: Luca Zidane; Rafik Belghali, Aissa Mandi, Ramy Bensebaini, Rayan Ait-Nouri; Ramiz Zerrouki, Ibrahim Maza, Houssem Aouar, Nabil Bentaleb; Riyad Mahrez, Fares Chaibi.
coach_comments_home: Murat Yakin said Switzerland had to respect every opponent, expected a difficult game, and emphasized creating and converting chances with conviction and quality. He also described Algeria as an interesting opponent with individual quality.
coach_comments_away: Vladimir Petkovic said Algeria had received celebration photos and videos from home, hoped the travelling support would give the team positive energy, and said Algeria would give everything for them.

expected_lineup_away: Confirmed Algeria XI: Zidane; Belghali, Mandi, Bensebaini, Ait-Nouri; Zerrouki, Bentaleb; Mahrez, Aouar, Chaibi; Maza.
coach_comments_home: Murat Yakin said Switzerland needed to respect every opponent, expected a difficult match, and stressed creating and converting chances with conviction and quality.
coach_comments_away: Vladimir Petkovic said Algeria had used Plans A and B in the group stage, might use Plan C against Switzerland, and that despite being Swiss his country for the match would be Algeria.

injuries_home: Ismael Kone out with a leg fracture and will miss the remainder of the World Cup; Alphonso Davies is returning from a hamstring injury and Jesse Marsch said Canada were deciding whether to use him from the start or off the bench.
injuries_away: Chadi Riad doubtful/fitness concern with a knock but reportedly returned to full training and was expected to be available barring a late setback; Nayef Aguerd and Abde Ezzalzouli were ruled out of Morocco's World Cup squad before the tournament because of injuries.
expected_lineup_home: Sports Mole predicted Canada XI: Crepeau; Johnston, Bombito, Cornelius, Laryea; Buchanan, Saliba, Eustaquio, Shaffelburg; David, Oluwaseyi.
expected_lineup_away: Sports Mole predicted Morocco XI: Bono; Hakimi, Diop, Riad, Mazraoui; El Aynaoui, Bouaddi; Diaz, Ounahi, El Khannouss; Saibari.
coach_comments_home: Jesse Marsch said Canada would consider using Alphonso Davies either from the start or off the bench, said Canada would not change its identity but would use tactical nuances, and described preparing for Morocco as a difficult task because they are very strong.
coach_comments_away: Mohamed Ouahbi said Brahim Diaz is important defensively and offensively, that Morocco try to create organised chaos through mobility, and told his players Canada would be Morocco's most challenging World Cup match so far.

injuries_home: Ismael Kone is out with a leg injury. Sports Mole also reported Canada had no other fitness concerns, with Alphonso Davies having returned from long injury absence as a substitute against South Africa.
injuries_away: Chadi Riad is a doubt/fitness concern after a knee knock against the Netherlands; Morocco coach Mohamed Ouahbi/Wahbi said it did not appear very serious, that Riad trained, and that only 100% fit players would play. Sports Mole listed Morocco's out list as none.
expected_lineup_home: Crepeau; Johnston, Bombito, Cornelius, Laryea; Buchanan, Saliba, Eustaquio, Shaffelburg; David, Oluwaseyi
expected_lineup_away: Bono; Hakimi, Diop, Riad, Mazraoui; El Aynaoui, Bouaddi; Diaz, Ounahi, El Khannouss; Saibari
coach_comments_home: Jesse Marsch said the match was not about revenge for Canada's 2022 World Cup loss to Morocco, saying his focus was on preparing for Morocco's strengths and imposing Canada's style.
coach_comments_away: Mohamed Ouahbi/Wahbi said he would not respond to the Canada coach's comments about Morocco being favorites, that Morocco respects Canada, and that past performances would not be enough to win; he also said only fully fit players would play.
